_help._comment: JSON does not support // comments, so these _help and _comment fields are real JSON fields. cv.exe ignores them.

capture_fields.mode: Selects active source: camera, video, pi-camera, screen, screen-virtual, or screen-region.
capture_fields.source: Video file path used when mode is video.
capture_fields.camera_index: Camera number used when mode is camera. Usually 0 for first camera, 1 for second.
capture_fields.width: Requested capture width for camera or pi-camera.
capture_fields.height: Requested capture height for camera or pi-camera.
capture_fields.fps: Requested capture FPS for camera or pi-camera.
capture_fields.screen_left: Left X position used when mode is screen-region.
capture_fields.screen_top: Top Y position used when mode is screen-region.
capture_fields.screen_width: Region width used when mode is screen-region.
capture_fields.screen_height: Region height used when mode is screen-region.

crsf_fields.device: Serial port, for example COM47 on Windows or /dev/ttyAMA0 on Raspberry Pi.
crsf_fields.baudrate: CRSF baud rate. Common value is 420000.

display_fields.fullscreen: Set to true to open the OpenCV preview window fullscreen.

examples.camera: Set capture.mode to camera and camera_index to 0.
examples.video: Set capture.mode to video and source to a video file path.
examples.screen_region: Set capture.mode to screen-region and adjust screen_left, screen_top, screen_width, screen_height.
examples.pi_camera: Set capture.mode to pi-camera, usually with width 640, height 480, fps 30.

capture._comment: Change only mode to switch active source. Other fields can stay here for later use.

crsf._comment: Leave device empty to disable CRSF.

display._comment: Set fullscreen to true on Raspberry Pi desktop autostart.
